perturbation.original_text: XLI grants WCC a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the registered trademark "Light Sheets" during the term of this Agreement in connection with national, regional or local print advertising, together with Internet, television, video and on-site advertising, for WCC and its Event(s) during the term of this Agreement, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof. This license shall terminate automatically upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, at which time WCC agrees to cease all use of the registered trademark "Light Sheets". In further consideration of this Agreement, WCC agrees that it shall not in any way disparage the brand name "XLI", nor any of XLI's parent, subsidiary, or affiliated companies or its or their products. XLI shall provide WCC with all the applicable logos for the "Light Sheets" trademark. XLI shall have the right of prior written approval over all uses of the trademark "XLI" by WCC. The parties understand and acknowledge the importance of protecting the goodwill associated with their respective trademarks. Consequently, WCC hereby assigns to XLI all goodwill and all other rights developed in connection with WCC's use of XLI's trademarks which shall inure to the benefit of XLI.
perturbation.changed_text: XLI grants WCC a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the registered trademark during the term of this Agreement in connection with advertising, for WCC and its Event(s) during the term of this Agreement, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof. This license shall terminate automatically upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, at which time WCC agrees to cease all use of the registered trademark. In further consideration of this Agreement, WCC agrees that it shall not in any way disparage the brand name "XLI", nor any of XLI's parent, subsidiary, or affiliated companies or its or their products. XLI shall provide WCC with all the applicable logos. XLI shall have the right of prior written approval over all uses of the trademark by WCC. The parties understand and acknowledge the importance of protecting the goodwill associated with their respective trademarks. Consequently, WCC hereby assigns to XLI all goodwill and all other rights developed in connection with WCC's use of trademarks which shall inure to the benefit of XLI.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the mention of 'Light Sheets' trademarks, creates an in-text contradiction, making the agreement less clear about what specific trademark WCC is licensed to use. The section still refers to 'the registered trademark' and 'XLI's trademarks', but because 'Light Sheets' has been removed, there is no trademark for WCC to be using in the first place, this makes it unclear exactly what trademark WCC is allowed to use and creates ambiguity regarding the assignment of goodwill and other rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XLI grants WCC a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the registered trademark during the term of this Agreement in connection with advertising, for WCC and its Event(s) during the term of this Agreement, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof. This license shall terminate automatically upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, at which time WCC agrees to cease all use of the registered trademark. In further consideration of this Agreement, WCC agrees that it shall not in any way disparage the brand name "XLI", nor any of XLI's parent, subsidiary, or affiliated companies or its or their products. XLI shall provide WCC with all the applicable logos. XLI shall have the right of prior written approval over all uses of the trademark by WCC. The parties understand and acknowledge the importance of protecting the goodwill associated with their respective trademarks. Consequently, WCC hereby assigns to XLI all goodwill and all other rights developed in connection with WCC's use of trademarks which shall inure to the benefit of XLI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific trademark 'Light Sheets,' creating ambiguity and contradicting the implied understanding that WCC is licensed to use this specific trademark. The remaining text refers to 'the registered trademark' and 'XLI's trademarks,' but without the explicit mention of 'Light Sheets,' it is unclear which trademark WCC is allowed to use. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, undermining the purpose and enforceability of the trademark license and subsequent assignment of goodwill. This qualifies as an Omissions - In Text Contradiction as essential language was removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: WCC shall ensure that all WCC public communications shall make reference to XLI products as "Light Sheets", as further defined in Attachment A [Sponsor Benefits Schedule] attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference.
perturbation.changed_text: WCC shall ensure that all WCC public communications shall make reference to XLI products, as further defined in Attachment A [Sponsor Benefits Schedule] attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the mention of 'Light Sheets', creates an in-text contradiction within Section 1.1.3. The changed text removes the specific requirement for WCC to refer to XLI products as 'Light Sheets' in its public communications. This change leads to an ambiguity because Attachment A (Sponsor Benefits Schedule) is expected to define this reference further, and the schedule still uses 'Light Sheets'. Without it this leads to WCC not actually refering the products by the appropiate name.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WCC shall ensure that all WCC public communications shall make reference to XLI products, as further defined in Attachment A [Sponsor Benefits Schedule] attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific requirement for WCC to refer to XLI products as 'Light Sheets' in its public communications. The contradicted text also states that WCC shall ensure that all WCC public communications shall make reference to XLI products, as further defined in Attachment A. The changed text's removal of 'Light Sheets' leads to a contradiction within the contract. Without it this leads to WCC not actually refering the products by the appropiate name.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the rights herein granted, and in keeping with XLI's desire to utilize WCC in the PROMOTION of its products and technology, XLI will pay WCC in accordance with Attachment B, which shall be deemed a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the rights herein granted, and in keeping with XLI's desire to utilize WCC in the PROMOTION of its products and technology, XLI will pay WCC.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the mention of 'Attachment B', creates an in-text contradiction that causes issues related to payment and services. While there is a description of payment and it is required, by removing the agreement payment, there is nothing stating the conditions in which XLI will pay WCC and the exact amount for those services. Since the attachment is not being mentioned, it implies that XLI will pay an arbitrary amount without a schedule.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the rights herein granted, and in keeping with XLI's desire to utilize WCC in the PROMOTION of its products and technology, XLI will pay WCC.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly refers to 'Attachment B' for payment terms, creating a clear and defined payment schedule. The changed text omits 'Attachment B,' leading to ambiguity and a lack of clarity regarding the payment schedule. This directly contradicts the implied expectation of a specific payment agreement, as outlined in the original text. The contradicted text is the changed text itself because it removes the reference, which is what causes the contradiction.
